139773.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that district and village councils make an important contribution to social cohesion and quality of life in the neighborhoods, districts and villages of the municipality of Tilburg. To do this, they must have a sufficient budget.

139774.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA sees the citizens' assembly (Tilburgerberaad) as an essential and innovative form of participation and consultation. The citizens' assembly helps to involve residents in the municipality of Tilburg, local democracy and important matters.

139775.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to gradually increase the property tax slightly in order to be able to invest more in districts, neighborhoods and facilities, such as accelerating housing construction, and more sports and recreational facilities. Tilburg has low housing costs compared to other municipalities.

139780.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Tilburg stands for a lively and liveable rural area and a sustainable future for farmers' lives. We are in support of plans that broaden business models. In larger developments, such as camping grounds, we also consider the impact on local residents and nature.

139781.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA accepts and respects the ruling of the Council of State on the development of Wijkevoort into a working landscape. If the court annuls the plan, no new large-scale business park will be added to the municipality. We are committed to restructuring existing sites.

139782.explanation: Recognized refugees must continue to be given priority for social housing. Refugees are not scapegoats for the housing crisis; this is due to years of failing liberal policy. That is why we are investing in additional social housing in Tilburg, and better chances of success for all home seekers.

139785.explanation: Tilburg has many companies that employ migrant workers – that is why proper living facilities are needed, close to work. We wish to prevent migrant workers from being exploited and/or ending up on the street. GroenLinks-PvdA is therefore in favour of good - and work-independent - housing complexes.

139786.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to continue planting more trees. We also need to protect existing greenery better. We are further tightening the tree ordinance - which regulates when trees may not be felled - in our plans. When felling is necessary, we plant many more trees to compensate.

139787.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA strives for the application of solar panels within existing urban areas first. Hectares of panels can still be placed on the roofs of industrial units, homes and vacant land. A solar park, such as the one on the noise barrier on the Kempenbaan/A58, is a good example.

139788.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to invest in a pleasant stay in the city centre, shopping centres, locations such as the Piushaven and Spoorzone, and city parks. This certainly includes good-quality, free toilet facilities. This is important for all our residents, but especially for people with disabilities.

139791.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants sufficient off-leash zones for dogs in parks, and in nature reserves where it can do no harm. We protect nature by not allowing dogs to roam free in places where biodiversity is high, and/or nature is vulnerable to disturbance.

139793.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes it is important that the city of Tilburg and local villages have a high-quality local broadcaster. A Media Fund has been set up for this purpose, fostering high-quality, critical, diverse, and pluriform reporting. Our aim is to continue investing.

139799.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants the Rotating House, like other art objects, to be better managed and maintained. We do pay close attention to the costs.

139800.explanation: City-wide events such as Carnival and the funfair must be publicly accessible to everyone. GroenLinks-PvdA wants all town squares and streets to be visited by Tilburgers of all ages. We also support locations specifically for young people up to the age of 17.

139802.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is in favor of a single extra cannabis café within Tilburg. However, a number of important conditions apply, such as distance to certain facilities (e.g. schools), parking, safety and accessibility. We will continue with the cannabis trial.

139803.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA sees cameras as a necessity for safety. They work preventively, and help with investigation. However, there are already many cameras in the centre of Tilburg. BOAs and police have not indicated a need for more, as it is already possible to take quick and adequate action.

139807.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is in favor of stimulating nature-inclusive forms of agriculture around the city and villages. We therefore allow less-intensive forms of livestock farming and herb-rich grassland on our leases. These contribute more to biodiversity than intensive cultivation.

139809.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants affordable rental housing for all Tilburg residents, and believes it should be a matter of course. By increasing the percentage from 30 to 40%, we increase the availability of affordable rental housing.

139812.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses to build homes within the existing built-up area and surrounding villages. This way, rural areas remain green and open. We are already proving that this is possible, and are dedicated to pursuing this policy.

139814.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA upholds the minimum size of 50m2 per home. Building more homes is not only about numbers, but also about pleasurable living conditions and affordability. Exceptions should be possible for specific projects (such as studios for students), and for people who want to live in smaller homes.

139815.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA was, is and remains opposed to a mandatory quid pro quo in welfare. We refuse to give up on people, and do everything we can to involve them in the labour market and society (e.g. through volunteer work). We work on the basis of trust, which works better than mandatory obligations.

139816.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA sees the value of pets. In Tilburg, there are a number of ways that contribute to covering veterinarian costs for residents with a low income. For example, through the Human Animal Emergency Foundation, the Tilburg support fund, or special assistance. We will continue with these methods.

139818.explanation: In recent years, GroenLinks-PvdA has successfully advocated for more community and youth workers, who help create liveable and safe neighborhoods and villages. They combat loneliness, and contribute to the mental health of young people. We want to continue this approach, and are not cutting back.

139821.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA sees narrowing of the road as a necessity for the construction of thousands of extra homes, for more/faster buses, and for residents' quality of life. Car traffic that does not need to be in Tilburg will be diverted. Without intervention, traffic will come to a standstill in the future.

139822.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants good air quality and a healthy Tilburg. Therefore, together with other municipalities, we have been working towards an environmental zone for a long time. We put the quality of life in districts and neighbourhoods first, and will not reverse this measure.

139823.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA sees that parking is important for residents. In recent years, the municipality has therefore often introduced paid parking in neighborhoods at the request of residents. The municipality has very affordable rates for residents in those neighborhoods. We will keep rates affordable.

139825.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes it is important for all districts, neighbourhoods and villages to be easily accessible by public transport. We want a train station for Udenhout, more regular and express cycle paths, more and faster buses, and also see the added value in a night bus.

139826.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports considering the need for an Islamic cemetery. The municipality now has a general cemetery, that is accessible to all religions. The available space will be under pressure in the coming years. That is why we wish to look ahead.

139828.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes it is important that the municipality of Tilburg work towards healthy outdoor spaces. That is why we aim to uphold the policy non-smoking policy at bus stops. This also prevents discomfort for other passengers.

139830.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants a suitable home for all. For a growing number of Tilburg residents, combined housing and care is a necessity. We are therefore in favour of building housing for the elderly, such as 'knarrenhofjes', where living and care can be combined.

139773.explanation: D66 wants to increase funding for neighbourhoods and villages, to strengthen local communities and improve liveability. This allows residents to take initiatives and invest in their own neighbourhoods, giving them responsibility and ownership over their living environment.

139774.explanation: D66 believes democracy is strongest when residents are actively involved. That is why we oppose abolishing the citizens’ assembly. Random selection ensures a representative cross-section of society, leading to better-informed decisions with broader public support.

139775.explanation: D66 wants to continue investing in the city while maintaining healthy municipal finances. For this reason, we are prepared to increase property tax, to enable continued investment in housing, sustainability and public facilities, ensuring a strong and resilient city for the future.

139780.explanation: D66 believes entrepreneurship is important, but it should be compatible with the surrounding area. That is why it is not always permitted. Through tailored decisions, we protect nature, landscape values, and quality of life, while giving people the flexibility to take initiatives where appropriate.

139781.explanation: D66 believes innovation and employment are essential to Tilburg’s future. That is why we are developing Wijkevoort – as the final new site – into an innovation campus, carefully designed for modern, sustainable businesses that fit into the surroundings and create jobs.

139782.explanation: D66 believes a quick start is crucial for successful integration. That is why we give recognised refugees priority access to social housing. This enables them to work, learn, build networks, and fully participate in society more quickly.

139785.explanation: D66 believes quality housing contributes to good living conditions for labour migrants. That is why we do not support large, one-sided housing complexes on the outskirts of the city, but instead favour small-scale locations. This helps prevent segregation and ensures better integration.

139786.explanation: D66 considers urban greening essential for a healthy and liveable city. That is why we strongly support robust compensation when trees are removed, with smart and effective replanting. This ensures more greenery in the right places.

139787.explanation: D66 aims to accelerate the energy transition while respecting landscape and nature. We therefore prioritise solar panels on rooftops, business parks and infrastructure. Use of agricultural land is only considered where it is carefully assessed and appropriate.

139788.explanation: D66 believes the city centre should be accessible to everyone. That is why we ensure there are sufficient free public toilets, making Tilburg welcoming for residents, visitors, older people, and people with disabilities.

139791.explanation: D66 seeks a balance between recreation and nature protection. That is why we provide clear rules and sufficient designated off-leash areas, allowing dog owners, other visitors, and nature to coexist in parks.

139793.explanation: D66 believes independent local journalism is essential for a strong democracy. That is why we support Omroep Tilburg. It ensures residents are well informed, and the organization strengthens people’s connection to their city.

139799.explanation: D66 believes that public art contributes to a city’s identity and sense of pride. That is why we continue to maintain iconic artworks, keeping Tilburg on the map as a city of creativity and innovation.

139800.explanation: D66 believes carnival is a celebration for everyone together. Young people and adults should celebrate side by side, strengthening social cohesion and avoiding unnecessary separation. At the same time, we do take measures to prevent underage drinking.

139802.explanation: D66 aims to maintain control over cannabis policy. We therefore allow space for well-regulated cannabis cafés where appropriate, thus reducing nuisance, improving oversight, and keeping criminal activity at bay.

139803.explanation: D66 believes safety is best achieved through prevention rather than surveillance cameras. That is why we invest in cooperation with city centre partners, the police, and early intervention, keeping our nightlife areas both safe and accessible.

139807.explanation: D66 aims for a future-proof agricultural sector. We therefore end land leases for intensive livestock farming once contracts expire, reducing emissions and creating space for nature and more sustainable forms of agriculture.

139809.explanation: D66 believes in mixed and liveable neighbourhoods. We therefore pursue a balanced blend of social housing, affordable owner-occupied and rental homes, and market-rate housing. This creates room for first-time buyers within the housing market. Moreover, there is no need for such a large social share.

139812.explanation: D66 aims to accelerate housing delivery. We therefore focus primarily on building within existing urban areas and townships, and only carefully expand at the fringes where necessary, increasing supply without compromising liveability.

139814.explanation: D66 believes housing quality matters more than floor space alone. We therefore allow smaller, high-quality homes where appropriate, making housing more affordable to a wider group of people.

139815.explanation: D66 believes participation and voluntary work can support personal development and self-confidence. We therefore encourage community work where appropriate, helping people build skills and improve their prospects.

139816.explanation: D66 believes support should be targeted and carefully designed. We therefore do not favour a general reimbursement for veterinary costs, but do allow for exceptions such as assistance dogs. We thereby ensure support remains fair and workable.

139818.explanation: D66 sees youth work as an investment in the future. That is why we uphold it, offering young people opportunities and mitigating problems in the long term.

139821.explanation: D66 aims for a liveable city with safe streets, while keeping Tilburg accessible as the city grows. We therefore prioritise fewer cars, more green space, safety, and sustainable mobility. Without these measures, the city would become congested.

139822.explanation: D66 believes clean air is essential for public health. That is why we continue to restrict high-polluting vehicles in the city centre, improving both liveability and health.

139823.explanation: D66 aims for a fairer distribution of public space. Where necessary, we will introduce paid parking and invest in parking garages. This helps keep parking accessible for existing residents, while creating space for greenery and liveable neighbourhoods.

139825.explanation: D66 wants everyone to get home safely and sustainably. That is why we invest in night-time public transport, making nightlife safer and improving accessibility for neighbourhoods and villages.

139826.explanation: D66 believes that everyone should be able to follow their faith and traditions. That is why we are open to an Islamic cemetery in Tilburg, and want to explore what would be needed to make it a reality. In this way, we ensure equal treatment and respect for everyone’s beliefs.

139828.explanation: D66 aims for clean air and a healthy living environment. That is why we keep bus stops smoke-free, protecting passengers and working towards a smoke-free generation.

139830.explanation: D66 wants senior residents to be able to live close to care. That is why we invest in shared housing models to keep care easily accessible, and to improve mobility in the housing market. In this way, we also combat loneliness.

139773.explanation: The neighbourhoods are where action is taken, not at city hall.

139774.explanation: The Citizens' Council is a sham consultation body. A placebo. Very few Tilburg residents participate in a Citizens' Council, and they cost a lot of tax dollars.

139775.explanation: The property tax absolutely wouldn't need to be raised if Tilburg politicians didn't waste so much tax money. If they worked more efficiently and professionally, the property tax could even be lowered.

139780.explanation: Give farmers the chance to do business.

139781.explanation: It's a nature reserve. If houses must be built there, they should be designed with respect for the Wijkevoort area.

139782.explanation: Absolutely no priority for refugees, otherwise our own young and old will have little or no access to affordable housing.

139785.explanation: No more large complexes for migrant workers on industrial estates than absolutely necessary, because we still have too many people on benefits who could also be working.

139786.explanation: If there's enough space, the LST is in favor of more greenery, provided the right tree species are used.

139787.explanation: Sacrificing fertile agricultural land to install solar panels is absurd.

139788.explanation: More clean public toilets, the LST has been asking for this for years.

139791.explanation: Lots of parks already have off-leash areas for dogs.

139793.explanation: Support only if it improves the quality.

139799.explanation: Draaiend Huis is a never-ending nightmare. Lijst Smolders opposed this hideously ugly house from the start. Repairs and maintenance have already cost four times as much as the original cost.

139800.explanation: Carnival is also a celebration for our young people.

139802.explanation: Definitely no more cannabis cafés within the ring roads – fewer if possible. The LST has long been calling for one or more drive-through weed cafés on the outskirts of Tilburg: the same concept as McDonald's in the Katsbogten industrial estate. Customers of cannabis cafés from the broader region or abroad would then no longer disrupt traffic in the neighborhoods or on our ring roads.

139803.explanation: Everything reasonable should be done to ensure the safety of our Tilburg residents.

139807.explanation: Only stop leasing if housing can be built on that land.

139809.explanation: 31% is already high, and at 40%, construction will not take place because the entire project would run at a loss.

139812.explanation: If quality of life deteriorates due to excessive construction, then you shouldn't do it. FULL = FULL. More effective construction is possible on the outskirts of Tilburg, Berkel-Enschot, Udenhout, and Biezenmortel.

139814.explanation: If there is market demand, why not?

139815.explanation: That would be fair to those who pay for the benefits – the hardworking people of Tilburg.

139816.explanation: People can already apply for special assistance.

139818.explanation: Young people need to be taught norms and values, and anything that can contribute even a little is welcome.

139821.explanation: The current government has decided to remove two lanes from the entire Ringbaan West. The LST did everything in its power to combat that decision. From now on, we will do all we can to reverse this terrible decision. The cold, soulless war on our cars is in full swing.

139822.explanation: Pure political theatre that contributes nothing to the environment.

139823.explanation: The LST will fight against the increasing cost and expansion of paid parking. The war on cars knows no bounds.

139825.explanation: Even if you splurge one night, you can still split a taxi with others. Drunk people on night buses have caused a lot of trouble and unsafety in the past, which is why they stopped it in the first place.

139826.explanation: Provided that everyone pays for everything themselves. No subsidies, etc.

139828.explanation: It shouldn't be banned on the streets.

139830.explanation: If this is what the elderly want, then they deserve it after a life of hard work.

139773.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants tax money to be spent wisely. Extra funding for neighbourhood councils is not a goal in itself. We prefer investing directly in safe, clean and pleasant neighbourhoods, and in listening closely to residents and councils.

139774.explanation: VVD Tilburg believes decisions should be made by the democratically elected city council. A citizens’ assembly slows decision-making and costs nearly €500,000. Tilburg needs decisive choices and action, so we prefer spending that money directly in the city.

139775.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants to take as little money from your wallet as possible. Higher property taxes make living more expensive. We prefer to critically review spending, so that Tilburg remains the city with the lowest living costs among the 100,000+ municipalities.

139780.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants to give farmers and other businesses opportunities to earn extra income. Activities such as farm shops or campsites strengthen rural areas, as long as they fit the surroundings and do not cause excessive nuisance.

139781.explanation: VVD Tilburg believes the city must offer space for businesses. Wijkevoort is needed to create that space. It provides jobs, economic growth and stronger links with education, with attention to nature and accessibility.

139782.explanation: VVD Tilburg believes everyone deserves a fair chance on the housing market. We oppose priority that is based solely on refugee status, and instead plan to tackle shortages by building 3,000 homes per year.

139785.explanation: VVD Tilburg supports proper housing for labour migrants in appropriate locations. Large-scale housing near business areas reduces pressure on residential neighbourhoods.

139786.explanation: VVD Tilburg aims to preserve green spaces without rigid regulations that block housing. We choose tailor-made solutions that balance greenery, housing, and accessibility.

139787.explanation: VVD Tilburg supports a common-sense sustainability policy. Solar panels should be an option if landowners want them, but never at the cost of forcing farmers to stop.

139788.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants a welcoming city centre. Businesses can offer free public toilets year-round in exchange for lower local taxes.

139791.explanation: VVD Tilburg chooses balance and common sense. Off-leash areas can coexist with nature if rules and enforcement are clear.

139793.explanation: VVD Tilburg believes public money should be spent carefully. Omroep Tilburg already receives substantial funding, while other local media outlets receive little or none. Additional subsidies would create unfair competition, and are therefore not desirable.

139799.explanation: VVD Tilburg believes tax money should be spent wisely. Maintaining the Draaihuis ('Rotating House') is costly, and delivers little value. That money could be better spent in our city. We are okay with renaming it the 'Stationary House.'

139800.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants carnival to be a celebration for everyone. We focus on safety and effective supervision at all locations, instead of segregating youth. This keeps carnival fun and safe for people of all ages.

139802.explanation: VVD Tilburg aims to reduce nuisance and crime, which requires clear regulations and effective distribution across the city. New cannabis cafés could also be situated at the edge of the city, such as drive-through locations.

139803.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants safer nightlife areas. Additional CCTV helps prevent violence and nuisance, and allows quicker intervention when incidents occur.

139807.explanation: VVD Tilburg supports entrepreneurs and family businesses. Decisions should be made on a case-by-case basis. An automatic ban could force farmers to stop entirely, or prevent business succession.

139809.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants to build homes faster. Fixed percentages slow down projects. We prefer a balanced mix of housing that improves both mobility and availability.

139812.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants to solve the housing shortage by building 3,000 new homes per year. This requires densification and building at the city’s edge. Limited municipal space means we do not have the luxury of choice – we must build wherever possible.

139814.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants faster housing construction. Smaller homes make construction more affordable and are suitable for first-time buyers, students, and singles, allowing for better use of limited space.

139815.explanation: VVD Tilburg believes work should be rewarded, and participation is the norm. A reasonable quid pro quo helps people stay active, and improves their chances of returning to work.

139816.explanation: VVD Tilburg believes the municipality should focus on its core responsibilities. Support should be provided through work, income and existing schemes, not by covering veterinary bills.

139818.explanation: VVD Tilburg prefers prevention over cure, and youth services help prevent problems later. Cuts would be unwise, though we do strictly monitor how funds are spent.

139821.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants smooth traffic flows. Ringbaan-West should retain four lanes to ensure safety and prevent through-traffic in local neighbourhoods. We will also divert through traffic via alternative routes, and restrict freight traffic.

139822.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants cleaner air and an accessible city. We support fair and workable regulations with transition periods and exemptions, so that residents and businesses are not unfairly affected.

139823.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants parking to remain affordable, and free where possible. Paid parking is not an end in itself, and should only be introduced when truly necessary.

139825.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants people to get home safely. Night buses are especially important during major events. Users should pay a surcharge, to keep costs manageable.

139826.explanation: VVD Tilburg believes the municipality should remain neutral. We provide general cemeteries for everyone. Islamic burial is already possible at Central Cemetery West.

139828.explanation: VVD Tilburg supports clean public spaces. A smoking ban at bus stops reduces inconvenience and protects public health.

139830.explanation: VVD Tilburg wants suitable housing for seniors, but does not favour rigid housing concepts. We prefer to focus on independent living for as long as possible, and bring parties together to create supported housing. If there is clear demand and suitable viability, the city could facilitate a Knarrenhof initiative.

139773.explanation: Neighbourhood and village councils should be given larger independent budgets, allowing for professional support and the ability to hire independent expertise, e.g. to obtain a second opinion on large and complex projects.

139774.explanation: The Tilburg Citizens’ Assembly already exists, and allows residents to directly contribute to important decisions. Through a lottery, 100 residents were selected who together form a fair representation of the city. This creates greater support, trust in politics, and balanced recommendations.

139775.explanation: The Property Tax (OZB) should be kept as low as possible. This can be achieved by handling public funds carefully and by ensuring efficient working practices across all municipal activities.

139780.explanation: We support farmers starting small shops or campsites on their farms. This reinforces their income, promotes rural life, and encourages local and often organic products.

139781.explanation: Wijkevoort must remain green and serve as a green buffer between the airport and Reeshof. This protects liveability, nature, and the health of local residents, while also preventing further urbanisation of the area.

139782.explanation: Only people with an urgency declaration should receive priority in housing allocation. All other applicants should join the regular 'Woning in Zicht' waiting list. Of course we wish to adhere to the applicable laws and regulations.

139785.explanation: Medium-sized short-stay complexes (maximum two years) with 24/7 management are a better option than housing in residential neighbourhoods. They allow more social monitoring and better and safer living conditions for labour migrants, without putting extra pressure on local communities.

139786.explanation: We oppose the fixed requirement that two new trees should be planted for every tree that is removed. Compensation can also take place in other ways. Moreover, a tree-protection regulation already exists to ensure careful management of tree removal, replanting, and greenery maintenance.

139787.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land are acceptable to us, but not in close proximity to residential areas and not on a large scale. Landscape quality, liveability, and a tailored approach should be leading considerations.

139788.explanation: Public toilets in public spaces are essential for older people. They increase mobility, help seniors remain independent when out and about, and make the city more accessible, inclusive, and comfortable for everyone.

139791.explanation: There are sufficient designated off-leash areas in forests. Allowing dogs to run freely in parks and nature reserves is therefore unnecessary. To protect nature, wildlife, and other visitors, off-leash dogs should be prohibited in these areas.

139793.explanation: Omroep Tilburg provides news and information to the local area. Whether additional support is desirable depends on how this role is carried out, its reach, and its added value for residents. We therefore take a neutral position.

139799.explanation: We oppose major additional spending on the Draaiend Huis. Only regular maintenance costs are acceptable; expensive investments are not justified.

139800.explanation: If young people themselves indicate that there is a need for this, we are open to it. At the same time, we greatly value celebrating Carnival together, as it unites people across generations.

139802.explanation: There are already enough cannabis cafés in the city centre. Adding more would only increase nuisance and attract visitors from outside Tilburg, leading to more congestion and additional pressure on liveability.

139803.explanation: More camera surveillance should be introduced in nightlife areas. This contributes to safety, helps prevent incidents, and supports enforcement by enabling faster intervention when needed.

139807.explanation: When the municipality leases land to farmers for livestock farming and a contract expires, each case should be assessed individually. In general we support renewal, unless there are compelling reasons not to do so.

139809.explanation: We should not build based on fixed percentages, but rather on the needs of each district or village. What matters is that every area has enough affordable and accessible housing. Whether this is owner-occupied, rental, or social housing depends on the area in question.

139812.explanation: Urban densification of cities and towns is not the only solution. Limited housing development can also take place on the fringes, provided it is done carefully and village character is preserved. A creative approach is also needed to identify suitable building locations.

139814.explanation: Homes smaller than 50 m² should, in our view, only be intended for single-person households or small studios. It should only be open for discussion if research shows a clear need in a specific neighbourhood. However, it is not our preference.

139815.explanation: People receiving social assistance should, where possible, perform useful community work. This fosters participation and self-worth, with appropriate exceptions for those who are unable to do so due to their circumstances.

139816.explanation: We oppose the municipality paying veterinary bills for people on a minimum income. Municipal funds should be used for people, not for pets. Support should remain focused on basic needs and the financial security of residents.

139818.explanation: Youth services are important, but also costly. We believe parents also have their own responsibility in this regard. Whether more or less funding should be allocated is difficult to assess, so we take a neutral position.

139821.explanation: Ringbaan West should remain a four-lane road. However, adjustments are needed to give priority to high-quality public transport such as high-speed buses, and to improve traffic flow.

139822.explanation: Abolishing environmental zones for freight traffic worsens air quality and harms public health, especially for seniors. Environmental zones are essential for a clean, liveable, and safe city centre.

139823.explanation: Introducing paid parking in more neighbourhoods is only acceptable to us if proven necessary by traffic counts, and if residents are given a say in the decision.

139825.explanation: A municipally funded night bus service on weekends is a good idea for nightlife visitors and young people. It provides a safe and affordable way to get home at night, and reduces the risk of people driving under the influence.

139826.explanation: We do not believe an Islamic cemetery in Tilburg to be necessary. This issue requires broader societal consideration, but at this time it is not a priority for us within municipal policy.

139828.explanation: We agree that smoking at bus stops should be prohibited. Passengers, including older people and children, should not be exposed to second-hand smoke. Smoke-free stops contribute to a healthier, cleaner, and more pleasant public environment for everyone.

139830.explanation: Ageing among both the general population and the elderly is increasing rapidly, along with the demand for care. We therefore need to build housing for older people, preferably in clustered or shared frameworks where care and social interaction come together, enabling people to remain independent and connected.

139773.explanation: Citizen participation is very important, and neighbourhood and village councils are essential for this process. To make these councils work well, they need sufficient funding.

139774.explanation: The Socialist Party greatly values citizen participation. Some complex issues affect the entire city, and for such issues the SP is in favour of a citizens' assembly. It would therefore be a bad idea to stop using this instrument.

139775.explanation: We are not in favour of increasing the property tax, but if the council needs funds for important matters such as healthcare and poverty reduction, we would consider a slight increase in property tax to be acceptable.

139780.explanation: We want a sustainable society. By allowing shops to be located close to farms, we reduce the distance between food production and buyers, and offer farmers an alternative source of income.

139781.explanation: Tilburg does not need a large-scale new industrial estate. That is why the SP has been opposed to the plans for Wijkevoort from the outset.

139782.explanation: This is necessary to ensure that the movement out of asylum-seeker centres does not come to a complete standstill. To this end, sufficient social housing must be built – the shortage is the main cause of the waiting lists for social housing, not the priority given to refugees.

139785.explanation: Integrating migrant workers into society is impossible with large-scale housing in only a few locations. That is why we are focusing on small-scale housing spread across the city. This is better for migrant workers and neighbourhoods alike.

139786.explanation: Much of Tilburg already lacks greenery. More green spaces are beneficial for people's health and well-being.

139787.explanation: Though solar panels on agricultural land are not an ideal source of sustainable energy, we need all sources to have enough green energy in our municipality.

139788.explanation: Every Tilburger should have easy and free access to toilets, not only in the city centre but throughout the entire municipality. This will make the city more accessible for everyone.

139791.explanation: It is important that there are safe places where dogs are allowed to roam free. These spaces must be clearly marked, making things better for both dogs and for people.

139793.explanation: It is in everyone's interest for there to be independent, high-quality journalism in our city, that takes a critical look at Tilburg municipal politics.

139799.explanation: The council must finally ensure that the Draaiend Huis actually turns for once. If that does not work out, we can discuss the matter again.

139800.explanation: Carnival is a festival of the whole city. That is why every town square must be accessible to all ages. This way, we ensure that carnival remains enjoyable for everyone.

139802.explanation: We support Tilburg's cannabis café policy. However, we must take local wishes into account. We therefore want no cannabis cafés in the middle of residential areas, and certainly not in disadvantaged neighbourhoods.

139803.explanation: We do not want cameras to replace the police. If it is demonstrated that a camera in a specific location can increase safety, then we are of course in favour of it.

139807.explanation: A large proportion of meat production is for export. We do not think this is a good thing. That is why we should focus more on environmentally friendly arable farming that is produced locally.

139809.explanation: For the SP, 40% is not enough; we want 60% of all new-build homes to be social housing. We need to eliminate the enormous waiting lists for social housing now. The SP wants everyone who is entitled to social housing to be able to find a good home quickly – though availability does depend on location.

139812.explanation: We always prefer inner-city development to city expansion, but there is currently a severe housing shortage. That is why we do not want to discount expansion out of hand, provided that facilities are properly organised.

139814.explanation: We want not only affordable housing, but also good-quality housing. That also means sufficient space.

139815.explanation: The SP does not want people to perform compulsory work in return for benefits. However, we do believe that people on social assistance who choose to contribute to society should be encouraged to do so.

139816.explanation: Pets are important for people's health, well-being, and social interaction. Unfortunately, vets are very expensive, so to make pet ownership possible for everyone, it is a good idea to subsidise vet bills for people on low incomes.

139818.explanation: Youth support workers have a huge number of tasks, for which they receive little money. This is despite the fact that they perform an essential role. Cutting back on funding would therefore be a very bad idea, we should be investing more in youth services.

139821.explanation: The SP believes it is important to reduce car traffic in the city. That is why we want to continue with the trial temporarily narrowing Ringbaan West to a two-lane road plus a bus lane. If it proves unsuccessful, however, we support returning to four lanes.

139822.explanation: High-polluting vehicles are harmful to people, animals and the environment. Banning these vehicles from our busy city centre is therefore a good idea.

139823.explanation: Paid parking should only be introduced where there is sufficient local support. Otherwise, it is simply a means of generating revenue at the residents' expense.

139825.explanation: The night bus is a safe way to get home late at night, and should make a return. It is not only useful for young people who go out, but also for those who have to work late. This way they don't have to ride their bikes while drunk, or get behind the wheel when tired.

139826.explanation: Everyone has the right to be buried in a manner that is in keeping with their beliefs. We have a large Muslim community in the city, who should also have that option.

139828.explanation: We want to discourage smoking as much as possible. A ban must be properly enforced, however – otherwise it is pointless.

139830.explanation: The SP is committed to community care homes: a place where people can grow old with their partner, even if one needs care and the other does not. With fresh food and familiar, trusted faces. In such community care homes, there is also a large communal area for residents of the home and the neighbourhood.

139773.explanation: Village and neighbourhood councils play a key role in new plans. Investing in these councils strengthens local democracy and increases citizen engagement.

139774.explanation: The Tilburg citizens' assembly plays a key role in decision making. Investment therein strengthens local democracy and ensures citizens are better involved and are being heard. Participants are randomly selected to ensure a fair representation of society.

139775.explanation: Tilburg is investing in people, animals, and nature. The Property Tax (OZB) is a tool to help us continue doing so.

139780.explanation: A farm shop or campsite offers new opportunities for livestock farmers who are ceasing operations. However, this form of recreation or business must not disrupt nature. Tilburg must therefore work with the farmer to determine what is possible, and ensure there is no disruption to nature or disadvantage to residents.

139781.explanation: Large-scale distribution centers take up too much space, even when they're labelled as an "innovation campus". Tilburg is therefore immediately cancelling its current plans for Wijkevoort. We're investing in the natural environment of the surrounding area instead of more industrial parks.

139782.explanation: The housing crisis is caused by the fact that housing has become a profit model instead of a fundamental right. This isn't the fault of refugees, but of years of flawed national policy. Without priority, refugees are forced to remain in asylum centers (AZCs) and are unable to integrate into society.

139785.explanation: By placing migrant workers literally on the margins of society, you deprive them of the opportunity for integration and make them more vulnerable. In Tilburg there is no place for companies that exploit migrant workers.

139786.explanation: Trees retain water, combat heat stress, purify the air, and provide a home for many animals. The PvdD supports a strict tree policy that protects existing trees, and if a tree is felled, it must be generously compensated for within the same area.

139787.explanation: Solar panels and greenery will become the norm on roofs, facades, noise barriers, and above parking lots. As long as there's still room for panels in such spaces, they should not be installed on agricultural land.

139788.explanation: Access to a toilet should be a given for everyone. Many more free, accessible restrooms should therefore be available in public spaces. During events like the Tilburgse Kermis funfair, all visitors should have quick, free restroom access.

139791.explanation: Tilburg must provide sufficient, spacious off-leash areas for dogs so they can run and play. Off-leash dogs can disrupt the welfare of wild animals in some areas (during the breeding season, for example). Dogs should be kept on a leash in those places.

139793.explanation: Strong, independent local media and regional broadcasters are crucial for residents. The media informs citizens objectively. That is something worth investing in.

139799.explanation: The Draaiend Huis is a Tilburg icon. It is now maintained to prevent dilapidation, but no longer requires millions in repairs. The "Standing House" will receive a new creative purpose, in collaboration with Tilburg's cultural sector.

139800.explanation: In our view, Carnival is a celebration for everyone. Everyone is welcome everywhere, regardless of age. However, alcohol consumption under 18 will be monitored and not tolerated, and we share responsibility for the young people who join in the festivities.

139802.explanation: Designating a new cannabis café location must be done carefully and subject to clear criteria. The PvdD believes a cannabis drive-through is a possibility.

139803.explanation: Tilburg already has so much camera surveillance in its nightlife district that citizens can be tracked almost to their homes. Cameras are a reactive form of security; the solution should be found in a preventive policy, aimed at behavoural change.

139807.explanation: Livestock farms have no place in leaseholds. Breeding, using, and killing animals in livestock farming is unethical and unsustainable. It comes with significant animal suffering and pollution. Where the livestock industry ends, there will be space for nature, housing, and plant-based agriculture.

139809.explanation: More affordable houses are needed. In neighborhoods where less than 30% of housing stock is social, new houses should include at least 50% social housing. In poorer area's, it's beneficial to have sufficient other forms of housing as well. This will create a better balance between housing types.

139812.explanation: We are committed to building homes within existing residential neighbourhoods. There is enough space in these neighbourhoods for expansion. Freed-up space in rural areas should be used primarily for nature, recreation, and plant-based agriculture, and only after that for housing.

139814.explanation: In order to live comfortably and pleasantly, space is essential. Despite the tight housing market, building ever-smaller homes at the expense of residents' enjoyment and well-being is not a solution.

139815.explanation: In Tilburg, residents and their needs are a focal point. If someone who is receiving social assistance is willing and able to volunteer, their efforts will be appreciated but not required.

139816.explanation: In Tilburg, we need to expand municipal services for people on low incomes. This includes financial assistance for low-income pet owners. Pet medical care shouldn't be a luxury.

139818.explanation: Street coaches and youth support workers identify potential problems and ensure accessible contact with young people (and their parents). In this way, they fulfill an important preventive role and avoid problems in the future.

139821.explanation: Tilburg is growing. Ringbaan West is intended for local traffic. Anyone who doesn't need to be there will have to use other roads to reduce congestion on Ringbaan West. This way, we ensure cleaner air, improved traffic flow, and more space for buses.

139822.explanation: The zero-emission zone ensures cleaner air in the city centre. For this reason, we should expand it instead of restricting it.

139823.explanation: It's important and fairer to introduce paid parking throughout the city. This way, users pay to occupy scarce public space with their cars. It also encourages increased use of bicycles and public transport.

139825.explanation: Frequent public transport should be available in all villages and residential areas, including nights and weekends. This contributes to safety.

139826.explanation: The Central Cemetery West already has Islamic graves. In our view, Tilburg has room for all types of cemeteries, and if needed, a dedicated Islamic cemetery.

139828.explanation: Tilburg must work towards a smoke- and vape-free generation. Thanks to the Party for the Animals, Tilburg already has a smoking and vaping ban at bus stops. Other zones should be added.

139830.explanation: The need for care is increasing. Elderly care should be organized on a small scale and locally, in a senior housing complex for example. These also provide room for their pets, which are often an important part of their lives.

139773.explanation: The CDA supports giving neighborhood councils more space and formal involvement, for example by using neighborhood budgets.

139774.explanation: The CDA chooses greater participation and involvement of residents in major plans; a citizens’ assembly is a good example of this.

139775.explanation: The CDA does not want to increase property tax (OZB) beyond the inflation rate.

139780.explanation: The CDA wants room for farming and other businesses. At the same time, we strive for a healthy balance between agriculture, nature, and recreation.

139781.explanation: The CDA supports the development of Wijkevoort. This is good for both employment and the local economy.

139782.explanation: The CDA wants to first allocate 35% of available homes to people from the same neighborhood or village. After that, priority in social housing goes to urgent cases, including divorcees and asylum seekers. We aim to maintain this policy.

139785.explanation: The CDA wants proper housing for labor migrants. We opt for collective housing in industrial parks, close to the workplace. Homes in residential areas remain for starters and families. The scope should match the location and respect the human scale.

139786.explanation: The CDA supports green cities. When trees are cut down, extra trees must be planted. By planting more than we remove, we strengthen greenery, improve the climate, and ensure that neighbourhoods remain pleasant places to live.

139787.explanation: The CDA supports sustainable energy with tailor-made solutions. A total ban on solar panels on farmland is excessive. Where suitable, it should be allowed, with thought for the landscape, nature, and the position of farmers.

139788.explanation: The CDA wants a welcoming city center. Free toilets make the city accessible for everyone. They help older people, children, and people with disabilities. They also help solve toilet shortages during public events like Carnival and the fair.

139791.explanation: The CDA believes a total ban is too strict. Dogs can run free if well regulated. Clear guidelines are needed to protect nature, other animals, and visitors. This keeps parks and nature areas pleasant for everyone.

139793.explanation: The CDA wants to strengthen Omroep Tilburg. A strong local broadcaster provides reliable news close to home. This increases involvement in the city and villages, and helps residents better understand what is happening.

139799.explanation: The CDA believes art in public spaces is important. The Draaiend Huis is part of the city. Stopping maintenance would harm the artwork. However, costs must be clear, and maintenance should be performed thoughtfully, keeping public support in mind.

139800.explanation: The CDA supports a separate area for young people up to the age of 17. This gives them a safe place to get to know carnival. At the same time, all official carnival landmarks, such as D'n Inhoal and et Subiet, should remain accessible for the whole family. Carnival is celebrated together.

139802.explanation: The CDA wishes to combat nuisance and drug tourism, and improve safety. Expanding the number of cannabis cafés in Tilburg is therefore not desirable. The focus should be on livable neighborhoods, street safety, and protecting the public realm.

139803.explanation: The CDA wants more camera surveillance in nightlife areas, to improve safety and help prevent violence. Surveillance is also needed in unsafe public places outside the city centre, so everyone can feel safe.

139807.explanation: The CDA gives farmers space to run their businesses. A ban on leasing land to livestock farmers is too strict. We seek a balance between agriculture, nature, and recreation. With tailor-made solutions, we will keep the countryside liveable.

139809.explanation: The CDA promotes mixed and robust neighborhoods. A fixed minimum of 40% social housing across all of Tilburg does not reflect differences between neighborhoods. That is why we prefer to focus on tailor-made solutions, with an average of 30% social housing and more room for affordable middle-income housing.

139812.explanation: The CDA mainly aims to build in existing neighborhoods and districts. But where expansion is possible, we are not opposed to it.

139814.explanation: The CDA believes subdivision can offer opportunities, but must not lead to overcrowding. Existing homes should not be split into units under 50 m² if public space cannot support it.

139815.explanation: The CDA believes it is important for everyone to take part in society. Meaningful work provides structure and helps people stay active. It makes people feel useful, stay connected with others, and take easier steps toward paid work.

139816.explanation: The CDA believes that people on a minimum income should receive help with essential veterinary costs. We want to achieve this by working with pet food banks, veterinarians with community rates, and foundations that support low-income pet owners.

139818.explanation: The CDA believes youth service work is very important. Youth work helps prevent problems, offers support, and gives young people opportunities. Cutting back would make problems worse, and cost more later. Investing in young people is investing in Tilburg’s future.

139821.explanation: The CDA wants Ringbaan-West to be used mainly by local traffic. Through-traffic should be routed around the city. The CDA has ensured that the road narrowing can be reversed, if traffic models prove to be incorrect.

139822.explanation: The CDA wants a healthy and liveable city center. The low-emission zone helps improve air quality. We believe in reliable government, and businesses have invested in sustainable choices. The regulations cannot simply be reversed.

139823.explanation: The CDA believes paid parking should not be expanded willy-nilly. Parking affects people’s daily lives. Only if there is clear support in the neighborhood should paid parking be an option.

139825.explanation: The CDA wants people to get home safely at night. That is why we support a night bus between the city centre, residential areas, and surrounding villages, especially on Fridays and during major events. This improves safety, accessibility, and liveability in Tilburg.

139826.explanation: The CDA believes everyone should be able to be buried with dignity. This applies to all our residents. An Islamic cemetery has a place within a city that supports religion and mutual respect.

139828.explanation: The CDA believes that bus stops should remain smoke-free. Many people wait together in a small space. A smoking ban protects children, young people, and non-smokers, and creates a healthier and more pleasant public environment.

139830.explanation: The CDA believes it is important for older people to live comfortably and safely. That is why we support housing solutions allowing older people to live together and care for one another. This supports communal, less lonely ageing, with care nearby.

139773.explanation: The local neigbourhood and village councils contribute a lot to letting the voices of residents be heard. They need to be able to do their job properly. Extra funding, better schooling, and advice can help them in this regard.

139774.explanation: This innovative tool allows citizens to be heard in a way that is otherwise impossible. We must continue this approach. We do see value in critically evaluating the process.

139775.explanation: The municipality must be frugal with their spending. It is not necessary to increase this tax, as we can still pay for everything without the increase. This would only increase the cost of living for our residents.

139780.explanation: Tilburg's surrounds can be enriched if we allow farmers to start small shops and recreational venues. It is an easy way to increase traffic to these areas.

139781.explanation: Wijkevoort is a difficult case for Lokaal Tilburg. For some residents it is very important to have recreational opportunities near the Reeshof. However, the development could greatly benefit industry. Currently the court is deciding on this, so Lokaal Tilburg does not have a stance at the moment.

139782.explanation: In all of the Netherlands there is a shortage of housing, in nearly all demographics. Giving one group priority over another is unfair. We must give every group a fair chance, including people who are already in the Netherlands.

139785.explanation: By creating housing at the edges of our municipality we can make sure migrant workers are housed in an environment that suits them. The economy of Tilburg really needs migrant workers to operate properly, which is why we support this idea.

139786.explanation: For years, Lokaal Tilburg has been pleading for more green facilities. We do want more trees, however 200% replanting is not always possible. We want to replace every removed tree as a minimum. Where possible, we are willing to invest in more trees.

139787.explanation: Solar panels should primarily be placed on the roofs of buildings. Rather than using up valuable land, we should invest in different energy sources.

139788.explanation: We need more public toilets in the city centre, in as many locations as possible. These locations do need to be suitable, however. Our priority is maintaining comfort levels in public squares, which includes – but is not limited to – public toilets.

139791.explanation: Dogs, just like humans, should be able to enjoy parks. However, they can also do this while on a leash. By keeping dogs leashed, we prevent dangerous situations and accidents.

139793.explanation: Journalism is extremely important. The government should be held to account. Journalists can also inform residents of what is happening in their neighbourhoods. The local news therefore deserves extra money.

139799.explanation: The 'Draaiend Huis' [Rotating House] has seen better days. It is time for it to make way for something new, something cheaper, that actually makes our city appealing. In this time of housing crisis, we should be investing in real homes.

139800.explanation: Minors should also be able to enjoy carnival. They deserve a decent location where they can enjoy themselves. As it is impossible for bars to check everybody's age, a separate square could be a fitting solution.

139802.explanation: Tilburg's existing cannabis cafés are already the source of a lot of nuisance. We first want this to be resolved before we allow any new cafés to establish themselves.

139803.explanation: In order to prevent unsafe and illegal activities, camera surveillance can help in the nightlife areas.

139807.explanation: Farmers enrich the economy of Tilburg. We should not deny them space in the municipality. If we need it for different purposes, like housing, it should still be a possibility to take back the land when contracts end.

139809.explanation: The municipality should offer space for all kinds of housing, as there is a shortage in every category. By maintaining strict percentages, we only make it more difficult to build more houses. We do need discretionary room to build what our inhabitants really need.

139812.explanation: The housing crisis demands creative solutions. These do not always slot easily into existing neighbourhoods and villages. We should be open to all solutions.

139814.explanation: For inhabitants that do not mind living in a smaller apartment, like students or migrant workers, this can be a fitting solution. We should not pre-emptively deny building these houses.

139815.explanation: We would like all residents to put effort into improving the society of Tilburg. We also want to motivate people who get social welfare to do so, however it should not be mandatory.

139816.explanation: Keeping a pet is a choice people make. This choice comes with costs, like veterinarian bills. Other people, who chose not to have pets, should not be forced to contribute to this financially.

139818.explanation: This amazing concept ensures our youth is involved. They will carry this with them for the rest of their lives. It ensures positive behavioural changes and a feeling of togetherness. We should support this as a municipality.

139821.explanation: The Ringbaan-West is a very important road for Tilburg. Cars need to be able to go over it quickly, which is facilitated by more lanes. Lokaal Tilburg is therefore in favor of keeping the four lanes that it has now.

139822.explanation: A low-emission zone is a good tool to increase the air quality in the city centre. We should, however, offer suitable exceptions, for example for small businesses. They should be able to reach their destination in their work vans. We are therefore neither for nor against this plan.

139823.explanation: Paid parking should be an instrument for preventing too many cars parking in a single area, which can even be a benefit for the people living there. However, we should consider where this is actually needed.

139825.explanation: Every neighbourhood and village in Tilburg should be accessible by night. A night bus can help, as it provides easy transport and safe routes to the city centre.

139826.explanation: We recognize the Islamic community's need for an eternal resting place. If we can find the space, Lokaal Tilburg is not against the idea. However, a lot of other things also need space, such as housing, so we will need to see if and where it fits.

139828.explanation: Public transport should be accessible to everyone. This also means we should look out for one another when using it. Lokaal Tilburg therefore believes it is appropriate to prohibit smoking at bus stops.

139830.explanation: The retirement homes from bygone days need to come back. It was a nice and future-proof way of housing senior citizens who want to live together, and giving them the care they need.

139773.explanation: ONS Tilburg believes that they should not only receive more funding, but also have greater decision-making power. After all, they know better what is happening in their neighbourhood or village.

139774.explanation: ONS Tilburg considers public participation to be very important. This can be achieved through a citizens’ assembly. Conditions such as travel reimbursement and childcare need to be better organised to enable everyone to participate.

139775.explanation: ONS Tilburg does not see the necessity of an increase. If it proves necessary to improve facilities in our neighbourhoods and villages, this can be discussed under certain conditions.

139780.explanation: ONS Tilburg thinks it is a good idea for farmers to modernize their operations, but this should be considered on a case-by-case basis and in consultation with the farmer and the local community.

139781.explanation: ONS Tilburg voted against this development and remains opposed to it. There are other ways to keep our economy running.

139782.explanation: The law requires that residents belonging to a special target group, such as divorced parents with children, unhoused people, etc., be given priority for housing. We must follow this law. ONS Tilburg believes that no one specific target group has more rights than another.

139785.explanation: If we build or allow building, we would prefer large residential complexes on the outskirts of the city. We are realistic; migrant workers are part of our society.

139786.explanation: ONS Tilburg considers it very important to make our neighbourhoods and villages greener. Everyone should be able to enjoy the beauty and cooling effect of a tree. This should be done in consultation with residents.

139787.explanation: ONS Tilburg prefers both housing and solar panels. Tilburg should think in terms of opportunities, and be careful with space.

139788.explanation: Why are we still talking about this? Just do it!

139791.explanation: There is a shortage of off-leash areas. ONS Tilburg believes that more areas should be designated. This way, we can actually protect vulnerable natural areas.

139793.explanation: ONS Tilburg is not against investing in local media, but we do advocate careful action, transparency, and clear choices. We have no indication that Omroep Tilburg is short of funds.

139799.explanation: It’s a lot of money. This is the only artwork we always talk about. Just let the Draaiend Huis turn again, and resume its role in the Tilburg debate.

139800.explanation: Public events like Carnival are for everyone. A separate square should be no problem, if young people and hospitality establishments feel the need for it.

139802.explanation: ONS Tilburg supports having more cannabis cafés, but only in locations where the inconvenience to nearby residents will be limited. A drive-through at an industrial estate is one example.

139803.explanation: ONS Tilburg is open to additional camera surveillance in the nightlife area if it demonstrably contributes to safety. It is not an end in itself, but a means, and always in combination with prevention, enforcement, and clear safeguards.

139807.explanation: ONS Tilburg supports the reduction of the intensive livestock industry. However, a dialogue is necessary so that farmers have good business alternatives.

139809.explanation: At ONS Tilburg, the focus is on people, not numbers. We prefer to opt for high-quality and affordable housing, rather than adherence to a specific figure. The bar can be raised.

139812.explanation: ONS Tilburg opts for considered use of space, with a preference for development within existing  neighbourhoods, districts, and villages. Especially in the villages, there is often still room to build in a way that suits the local character, without unnecessarily encroaching on the landscape.

139814.explanation: Smaller homes are fine for specific target groups, such as students or care housing.

139815.explanation: We trust that all people want to make a meaningful contribution to our society. Let them do so freely, and save on the costs of monitoring everything. Act on trust.

139816.explanation: ONS Tilburg believes that support for veterinary costs does not need to be a standard right. Targeted assistance can, however, contribute to animal welfare, social cohesion, and the owner’s mental health, with clear criteria and tailored measures.

139818.explanation: Simply a foolish idea. Youth support work prevents the need for care and strengthens social cohesion. We must always continue to invest in young people.

139821.explanation: We are very curious about the results of the evaluation. Thanks to the motion by ONS Tilburg, through-traffic is being addressed in adjacent neighbourhoods, in cooperation with residents.

139822.explanation: ONS Tilburg is against abolishing the environmental zone. Clean air is important for public health. Moreover, this aspect is partly national policy, and cannot simply be reversed locally. The implementation must, however, be fair, with support and tailored measures.

139823.explanation: ONS Tilburg is against expanding paid parking. It should not be treated as a cash cow, or put extra pressure on neighbourhoods that are already under strain. First, we need to consider liveability, public support, and real problems.

139825.explanation: Yes! Young people should be able to get home safely after a night out.

139826.explanation: ONS Tilburg supports an Islamic cemetery. Being able to bury someone according to their own faith is part of a dignified farewell. It contributes to inclusion and shows that everyone, even in death, forms part of our community.

139828.explanation: ONS Tilburg supports the continued ban on smoking at bus stops. It protects the health of travellers, especially children and young people, and sets a clear example in public spaces: together, we ensure a healthy city.

139830.explanation: ONS Tilburg supports investing in new housing options where elderly people can live together with proper care. Such frameworks promote independence, social interaction, and appropriate care while preventing loneliness, thus making care personal and humane.

139773.explanation: VoorTilburg013 opposes giving more money to councils that already get a lot. We want fair distribution and younger members so youth can join in. Fresh ideas and using social media help create more neighbourhood support.

139774.explanation: VoorTilburg013 values citizens’ assemblies. They give residents a voice and bring in fresh ideas. Together, we make better decisions. Citizen participation strengthens trust and engagement in Tilburg’s policies.

139775.explanation: VoorTilburg013 opposes a quick property tax increase. If truly needed, it should be raised gradually with clear communication, so residents can adapt. No sudden extra costs, and for 2026 we want no increase at all.

139780.explanation: VoorTilburg013 supports farmers opening a shop or campsite. It creates jobs, promotes local produce, and strengthens local businesses. This keeps rural areas lively and attractive for everyone.

139781.explanation: VoorTilburg013 wants no businesses in Wijkevoort. This plan has dragged on for 24 years and wasted money. We choose to protect nature and stop waste. Green spaces matter more than new companies.

139782.explanation: Refugees with permits do need housing quickly, but there is a major shortage. Many young people, starters and divorcees also wait for years. VoorTilburg013 wants a fair balance throughout all groups.

139785.explanation: Migrant workers need housing, but not at the expense of Tilburg residents. If complexes are built, VoorTilburg013 wants proper facilities to prevent boredom and disturbance, and a healthy balance for all groups.

139786.explanation: An old tree is worth far more than two young ones. It stores CO₂, gives shade, supports biodiversity, and nourishes via root systems. VoorTilburg013 wants the city to focus on preserving existing trees, not just replanting.

139787.explanation: VoorTilburg013 believes farmers should decide for themselves if they want solar panels on their land. It supports green energy, and would generate extra income. The city should not ban it, but set out clear regulations to protect nature and the landscape.

139788.explanation: VoorTilburg013 wants adequate public toilets, especially for the elderly, women, children, and others with special needs. Not on every square, but certainly at busy spots and during events. Comfort without unnecessary costs.

139791.explanation: Safety and nature matter, but a full ban is going too far. VoorTilburg013 wants clear regulations: dogs off-leash only in designated areas, not in vulnerable nature zones. This way people and dogs can enjoy themselves without causing harm.

139793.explanation: Omroep Tilburg already receives federal and municipal support. VoorTilburg013 can see improvement: it offers student opportunities and runs public media. Extra funding makes sense only if visibility and impact for residents continue to grow.

139799.explanation: Culture matters, but not like this. The Draaiend Huis costs a lot, is a  white elephant, and brings little value to residents. VoorTilburg013 wants funds to go to visible projects that foster talent development and truly benefit Tilburg.

139800.explanation: A separate square for youth is not necessary. VoorTilburg013 wants them to join carnival safely, with options like extra festivities and no barriers on town squares. Everyone should celebrate together, with consideration for young people.

139802.explanation: Our city does not need any more cannabis cafés. Past failures in Berkel-Enschot, Tilburg North and West were wasted effort. VoorTilburg013 stresses resident and business input, and attention to safety and liveability.

139803.explanation: Extra cameras must be well distributed to ensure coverage of nightlife areas and key hotspots. VoorTilburg013 supports adding more if needed for safety. Focused use, not blanket implementation.

139807.explanation: Farmers should be able to rent land even with livestock. VoorTilburg013 wants the city to monitor farm size and maintain balance with nature and liveability. Don’t stop renting, but make clear agreements and protect our agricultural businesspeople.

139809.explanation: VoorTilburg013 disagrees with 40% social housing. Demand for affordable homes is about two-thirds of new builds, with equal need for affordable purchase and rental. Tilburg must aim for a broad mix, not just more social housing.

139812.explanation: VoorTilburg013 believes building should focus on existing neighbourhoods in the city, but villages like Berkel-Enschot, Udenhout and Biezenmortel could expand beyond their current borders. This ensures growth while preserving liveability.

139814.explanation: New homes under 50 m² are very welcome. VoorTilburg013 supports tiny homes as they are quick to build, energy-efficient, and can be bio-based. They foster social contact, fight loneliness, and promote sustainability and community.

139815.explanation: Many on welfare already do caregiving or volunteer work. A large number have medical or mental health issues, and cannot engage in quid pro quo. Only a small percentage commit fraud. Focus on tailored support instead.

139816.explanation: Pets help fight loneliness, but VoorTilburg013 believes the city should not pay all vet bills. Support should only go to those who truly need it, with clear pricing agreements. Consider covering check-ups as tailored solutions, and discuss unforeseen costs as they arise.

139818.explanation: VoorTilburg013 sees youth support work as essential. It is preventive, builds trust, and supports young people in their world, far better than the police or healthcare systems do. They work in streets, schools, and online. Cutting funds makes things worse. Youth is our future.

139821.explanation: VoorTilburg013 supports narrowing Ringbaan West while keeping it open for local traffic and public transport, to improve health and safety. Measures to redirect traffic are needed, and are already in preparation.

139822.explanation: VoorTilburg013 wants to maintain a healthy city centre, but businesses need time to invest in cleaner transport. Removing the environmental zone is not the answer; allow gradual transition with clear agreements.

139823.explanation: VoorTilburg013 supports paid parking in more neighbourhoods due to building objectives and new parking garages. Free short-term parking for shopping, sports and social facilities is essential to balance accessibility and liveability.

139825.explanation: VoorTilburg013 sees demand for a night bus, but past trials have failed due to low use and disturbances on the bus. If safety and communication improve, we would consider it. Accessibility matters.

139826.explanation: Part of Hoflaan in Tilburg West is already reserved as an Islamic cemetery. VoorTilburg013 believes the city should first assess the actual need and available space before making any further decisions.

139828.explanation: VoorTilburg013 wants to uphold the smoking ban at bus stops, to prevent litter and protect children and non-smokers. We want to put health and liveability first, without turning the city into a nanny state.

139830.explanation: VoorTilburg013 wants to invest in housing where seniors can live and receive care together. As the elderly population itself ages, this is essential. Culture-focused concepts like Zandstaete strengthen social ties, improve quality of care, and promote dignity in old age.

139773.explanation: FVD Tilburg vindt dat er geen klakkeloos geld overgemaakt moet worden aan de Wijk- en Dorpsraden. Wel zijn we voorstander dat deze raden onafhankelijk te werk kunnen gaan.

139774.explanation: FVD Tilburg vindt een referendum de beste vorm van burgerparticipatie, een burgerberaad heeft niet dezelfde impact.

139775.explanation: FVD Tilburg vindt lastenverhoging voor inwoners onacceptabel. In plaats daarvan willen wij sturen naar lastenverlagingen door te kijken naar de uitgaven van de lokale overheid.

139780.explanation: FVD Tilburg vindt dit acceptabel, het gaat immers om lokale bedrijvigheid te stimuleren of te starten op (vaak) eigen grond.

139781.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat de Reeshof de dupe is geworden van de plannen rondom Wijkevoort, op zijn minst is het bouwen van woningen op deze locatie een betere toevoeging dat grootschalige logistieke blokkendozen.

139782.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat woningen die in Tilburg staan of gebouwd gaan worden naar Tilburgers gaan, en absoluut niet naar vluchtelingen die geen binding hebben, cultureel, economisch of sociaal, met Tilburg.

139785.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat het bouwen van grote wooncomplexen voor arbeidsmigranten géén taak is van de gemeente en tevens onwenselijk is voor de Tilburgers die een woning aan het zoeken zijn.

139786.explanation: Ondanks FVD Tilburg kritisch is en tegen het drammerige klimaatbeleid van deze gemeente is, begrijpen wij wel dat bomen óók een bijdrage leveren aan het welzijn en de leefomgeving van onze inwoners.

139787.explanation: FVD Tilburg wil aansturen op daadwerkelijke groene energie, zoals kerncentrales, Thorium en SMR's. Zonnepanelen zijn niet efficiënt, draaien op subsidies en bevatten óók chemische en vervuilende stoffen

139788.explanation: FVD Tilburg begrijpt de vraag voor meer openbare toiletten, maar de binnenstad in een openbaar urinoir veranderen, vinden wij niet verstandig.

139791.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dit ''betutteling'' vanuit de overheid.

139793.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat media onafhankelijk moet blijven, als de overheid geld gaat geven aan journalistiek, hoe kunnen zij dan nog de ''waakhond'' zijn van de politiek?

139799.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat het Draaiend Huis weg moet. Het is al jaren een doorn in het oog, het slurpt belastinggeld op en is een perfect voorbeeld van falend (cultuur)beleid.

139800.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat Carnaval voor iedereen openbaar en toegankelijk moet zijn.

139802.explanation: FVD Tilburg is niet tegen nieuwe coffeeshops, mits deze passend zijn en geen overlast veroorzaken in de buurt waar deze mogelijk komen te staan. Denk bijvoorbeeld aan een ''Drive-Through Coffeeshop''.

139803.explanation: FVD Tilburg vindt cameratoezicht een inbreuk op de privacy van Tilburgers en is een ''schijnveiligheid''.

139807.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat de gemeente zich absoluut niet moet bemoeien met voedselvoorziening of grond van boeren.

139809.explanation: FVD Tilburg pleit voor het bouwen van koopwoningen en het stimuleren van betaalbare koopwoningen.

139812.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat er woningen gebouwd kunnen worden aan de rand van de stad of in een ander gebied, mits dit passend is voor de omgeving. De Binnenstad en binnen de Ringbaan beginnen langzamerhand dicht te groeien.

139814.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat ''woningen'' die kleiner zijn dan 50 vierkante meter mensonterend. Dit soort huizen belemmeren het starten van een gezin en werken negatief op het welzijn en de leefomgeving van mensen.

139815.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat met deze regeling er op zijn minst een positieve bijdrage wordt geleverd aan de samenleving.

139816.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat de gemeente niet de rekening moet betalen voor een persoonlijke keuze.

139818.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind jongerenwerk belangrijk, maar zoals het nu is ingericht, verdwijnt er veel geld aan bureaucratie en komt er weinig bij diegene die het het meest nodig hebben: de jongeren.

139821.explanation: FVD Tilburg pleit voor het behoud van de vierbaansweg.
