139822.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind deze, en soortgelijke klimaatplannen, onzin, want dit soort maatregelen raken ondernemers, ouderen- en jongerenvervoer en eenieder die Tilburg wilt bezoeken met de auto.

139823.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat betaald parkeren invoeren extra lasten zullen brengen aan onze Tilburgers. De automobilist in Tilburg is géén melkkoe.

139825.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat dit géén taak is van de gemeente.

139826.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dit géén taak van de gemeente om te faciliteren.

139828.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat de gemeente zich niet moet gaan bemoeien over wat Tilburgers tot zich nemen.

139830.explanation: FVD Tilburg vind dat er inderdaad woonplekken moeten komen waar ouderen samen verzorgd kunnen worden, maar dit kan op eigen initiatief, of instellingen en coorporaties, niet als gemeentelijke impuls.

139773.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB supports neighbourhood and village councils because they have the best understanding of local needs. If additional funding is needed for these bodies, BBB Tilburg-BUB will support it.

139774.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB considers the citizens' assembly a sham democracy, and instead wants greater involvement of neighbourhood and village councils in municipal policy.

139775.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB generally supports lowing municipal taxes, so that property tax does not need to be increased and can, in fact, be lowered in the long term.

139780.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB is, of course, in favor of this. We want to simplify regulations for businesspeople, farmers included.

139781.explanation: This is a rather nuanced matter: the municipality previously purchased the land under Wijkevoort, meaning it will be an expensive natural area indeed if nothing is done with it. However, BBB Tilburg-BUB is critical of new industrial estates.

139782.explanation: No, BBB Tilburg-BUB wants the municipality to prioritise its own residents for social housing.

139785.explanation: Quality of life for our own residents is our top priority. Such complexes could potentially lead to nuisance and safety risks.

139786.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB supports nature conservation. We are therefore against felling trees in general, unless they are diseased or pose a threat to the surroundings.

139787.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB does not believe that solar panels belong on agricultural land.

139788.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB supports more free public restrooms, but they don't need to be installed on every town square.

139791.explanation: Agreed, but more space should be created for off-leash areas.

139793.explanation: The municipality of Tilburg should critically examine subsidy allocation.

139799.explanation: This 'art project' has already cost Tilburg far too much money.

139800.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB is against a nanny state. We will leave this to the market.

139802.explanation: The municipality of Tilburg has enough cannabis cafés.

139803.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB opposes more cameras in the city – which only create a false sense of security – and advocates for more visible police on the streets.

139807.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB is not in favour, because of the loss of land security for farmers.

139809.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB strives for a healthy mix of affordable owner-occupied homes, mid-range rentals, and social housing.

139812.explanation: Only if necessary should the municipality of Tilburg be permitted to expand into surrounding areas.

139814.explanation: Fine, as long as the homes are allocated to students, singles, or elderly people. The municipality of Tilburg must build a balanced approach for families, single people, young people, and seniors.

139815.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB wants everyone to make an appropriate contribution to society.

139816.explanation: This is not the municipality's responsibility.

139818.explanation: Youth support work aids young people in their personal development and participation in society; we shouldn't cut back on it. In the long term, we want to involve parents and make them jointly responsible in the process, depending on the situation.

139821.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB opposes narrowing Ringbaan-West, and believes that Tilburg must remain accessible to everyone.

139822.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB opposes a zero-emission zone or diesel ban. Accessibility is not a luxury, but a necessity.

139823.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB is not in favour. Accessibility is a necessity, not a luxury.

139825.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB supports a night bus because it ensures that residents living in neighbourhoods and villages return home safely.

139826.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB believes that the municipality of Tilburg should carefully manage the available space.

139830.explanation: BBB Tilburg-BUB believes that balanced construction is necessary, with special attention to small-scale housing for seniors that includes appropriate care.

139773.explanation: Social cohesion is a key condition for strong neighbourhoods, where people look out for one another and overall liveability is higher. Neighbourhood and village councils can contribute even more to this by continuing to invest in it

139774.explanation: Citizens’ assemblies bring councils and citizens closer together and help strengthen democracy. It is essential that governments and politicians listen to citizens, and that residents have real influence over decisions that affect them.

139775.explanation: The municipality of Tilburg is in a healthy financial position. We continue to look ahead, but at this moment we see no need to place a greater tax burden on residents than necessary.

139780.explanation: Volt advocates for a sustainable business model for farmers. A shop or camping site can be helpful in this regard. But a shop or camping site is not suitable for every location.

139781.explanation: The Tilburg City Council adopted the Wijkevoort Work Landscape zoning plan in 2021. Clarity, certainty and continuity are important to Volt. Existing plans must be preserved as much as possible.

139782.explanation: The demand for housing is high and the market is tight. Refugees with a residence permit make up only a small share of all people seeking housing. Removing their priority status does not solve the housing shortage. Building more homes is essential to relieve the pressure.

139785.explanation: Volt supports the development of decent housing near the workplaces where labour migrants are employed. These locations are often situated on the outskirts of the city, close to industrial areas.

139786.explanation: More greenery in the municipality is essential to reduce heat stress and prevent flooding. We apply the 3‑30‑300 rule. Trees should only be removed for valid reasons, and as long as we have not yet met this standard, Volt considers compensating each felled tree with two new ones appropriate.

139787.explanation: Volt wants to align with national policy of the ministry, provinces and municipalities. Solar panels are permitted on roofs, parking lots and along roads. But in principle, they are not permitted on agricultural land.

139788.explanation: Tilburg is continuously working on urban renewal to make the city centre more attractive. It should be easy and free for visitors to access public toilets

139791.explanation: Nature and green spaces are a key priority for Volt. Dogs are welcome, but must always be kept on a leash by their owner. This helps ensure these areas remain pleasant for everyone and allows owners to remove waste immediately. Volt does support clearly designated off-leash areas.

139793.explanation: Volt is committed to strengthening regional and local media. However, financial support from the municipality must be in the interest of all citizens.

139799.explanation: The Turning House is a work of art of national importance and a symbol of Tilburg’s courage and ambition. It must not be lost.

139800.explanation: At major events in the city, there should always be space for young people under the age of 18. During Carnival, for example, a dedicated area is set up specifically for youth

139802.explanation: Legalisation, regulation, evaluation. Volt supports the legalisation of cannabis and hash. Transparent regulation and continuous evaluation are essential in this process. Preventing the opening of new coffeeshops can be counterproductive.

139803.explanation: We want a safe city with less crime and nuisance. Camera surveillance can help achieve this, but we use it only when it is genuinely necessary and never for longer than required.

139807.explanation: Volt is not against livestock farming. However, we do support making intensive livestock farming more sustainable. Community land can be leased by livestock farmers, but the land must be used sustainably.

139809.explanation: Volt supports the policy of working towards 30% social housing. If there is no need for a higher share, we should not pursue it. Developing housing in higher segments can lead to a more favourable financial outcome, enabling higher‑quality public space design.

139812.explanation: The focus should be on densification within the existing urban fabric. However, locations outside current neighbourhoods should not be ruled out as long as the demand for new housing remains high.

139814.explanation: Within the Housing Standard, there are housing types smaller than 50 m². Volt does not want these to become the norm, but does want to allow exceptions. As the number of single‑person households continues to grow, each project area should be assessed to determine whether these housing types are suit

139815.explanation: Welfare benefits is intended to provide subsistence living for people who cannot support themseleves. This is what society is allowed to ask from a government. No work is required in return.

139816.explanation: A pet is important to many people. But this also comes with responsibilities for the owner. It is not the responsiblity for the government to pay medical costs of pets.

139818.explanation: Volt doesn't want to cut back on youth work. It is an important task for the local government. And young people are the future. We have to invest in this.

139821.explanation: The decision to convert the Ringbaan-West into a two-lane road was made by the City Council end of 2025. An evaluation will take place 18 months after completion. Volt wants to wait on the outcomes of this evaluation.

139822.explanation: Volt's vision is a greener, low-traffic city center with less noise nuisance and air pollution. Therefore, the low-emmission zone must remain.

139823.explanation: Volt is committed to reducing traffic in the neighbourhood and increasing public green space. Parking spaces come at the expense of public space and livability. There should be compensation for this. This can also encourage more use of for example bike or public transport.

139825.explanation: Together with the province, we want to explore improved options for night-time public transport, for example through a combination of bus and rail services. Bus connections to and from neighbourhoods and villages are an essential part of this

139826.explanation: In Tilburg, there should be opportunities for everyone in all aspects of life, with full respect for religious freedom. The municipality is already in dialogue with the Muslim community about a dedicated Islamic cemetery, and Volt supports this initiative

139828.explanation: Smoking is already prohibited at bus stops, and rightly so. Bus stops are often used by groups of people who should not be exposed to nuisance or health risks caused by others smoking while waiting

139830.explanation: The local government, along with other parties, should invest more in housing development. Senior citizens are a specific target group. If more assisted living housing is built, this will also ensure more fluidity in the housing market.

139773.explanation: Neighbourhood/village councils increase residents’ involvement and influence. More funding means more room for initiatives from the community.

139774.explanation: The citizens’ assembly strengthens democratic participation. The benefits of citizen input outweigh the objections to selection by lottery.

139775.explanation: DENK Tilburg wants to keep housing affordable. Increased financial burdens for residents and businesses must be prevented; lower incomes must be protected.

139780.explanation: Farmers deserve space for additional income activities, but not at the expense of liveability, nature, and the environment. A balance of interests is necessary.

139781.explanation: Wijkevoort is a nature zone. Development would damage the landscape and lead to increased traffic and pollution.

139782.explanation: People with a residence status have a legal right to housing and must be able to integrate. Priority housing prevents problems in refugee accommodation and homelessness. The housing crisis is not the fault of refugees.

139785.explanation: Housing is needed, but large-scale complexes must not become isolated enclaves. Quality and distribution are the main considerations.

139786.explanation: Green space is essential for liveability and health. Compensation must be at least one-to-one.

139787.explanation: Roofs and business parks should be used first. Agricultural land should only be used in cases of high necessity, and with due consideration.

139788.explanation: Accessible toilets in public areas are a basic facility for everyone.

139791.explanation: Protecting nature is important, but there must also be clearly designated off-leash areas.

139793.explanation: Strong local media is crucial to democracy and social cohesion.

139799.explanation: This artwork is expensive and lacks public support. That money could be better spent on neighbourhood facilities.

139800.explanation: It is unlikely that young people will comply with this. Enforcement measures and spreading out crowds will be more effective.

139802.explanation: Cannabis cafés normalise drug use and cause nuisance, particularly in vulnerable neighbourhoods.

139803.explanation: Additional surveillance helps limit nuisance and drug-related crime.

139807.explanation: The Netherlands has too much livestock farming. Municipal land should be used for more sustainable and future-proof purposes.

139809.explanation: This is necessary to address the housing shortage fairly.

139812.explanation: This is insufficient to solve the housing crisis.

139814.explanation: This is necessary for starters and single-person households. Houses should be affordable and of good quality.

139815.explanation: Social benefits are a safety net, not a punishment. Mandatory quid pro quo increases pressure and displaces regular work. Voluntary participation with guidance works better.

139816.explanation: Animal care should not be a luxury. Municipal support prevents animal suffering and financial problems for people on low incomes.

139818.explanation: Youth support work is preventive and strengthens opportunities. Cutting funding is short-sighted and leads to higher social costs.

139821.explanation: Ringbaan-West is a major traffic artery. Four lanes ensure traffic flow and prevent cut-through traffic in residential neighbourhoods.

139822.explanation: The low-emission zone mainly affects people on low incomes. Sustainability measures must be feasible and socially just, not punitive.

139823.explanation: Paid parking increases costs for residents and visitors. Parking in residential neighbourhoods must remain affordable and accessible.

139825.explanation: A night bus improves safety and accessibility, especially for people without a car or with irregular working hours.

139826.explanation: Equal rights also mean space for religious cemeteries, which have a place in an inclusive city.

139828.explanation: Bus stops are public spaces. Protecting the health of passengers and workers comes first.

139830.explanation: Communal living prevents loneliness and helps older people remain independent for a longer period.

statements.title: During Carnival, there should be a separate area for children aged 17 and under.
Ringbaan-West should remain a four-lane road.
The municipal property tax (OZB) should be raised.
statements.moreInfo: De onroerendezaakbelasting (ozb) is een belasting van de gemeente. Deze belasting is voor gebouwen zoals woningen en winkels. Eigenaren van woningen betalen deze belasting. Bij bedrijfspanden betalen de eigenaren en soms ook de gebruikers. De hoogte van de ozb is niet in elke gemeente hetzelfde. De gemeenteraad bepaalt hoeveel ozb mensen moeten betalen. De gemeenteraad beslist ook waar de gemeente het geld van de ozb aan uitgeeft.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the building of new homes that are smaller than 50 square metres.
Neighbourhood and village councils should receive more funding.
statements.moreInfo: Tilburg zijn er 22 wijk- en dorpsraden. Hoeveel geld zij kunnen uitgeven hangt af van subsidies die zij van de gemeente krijgen. Bewoners kunnen ideeën aandragen en de gemeenteraad beslist of ze daar subsidie voor krijgen. De wijk- en dorpsraden beslissen zelf mee. Ze krijgen dus niet elk jaar een vaste hoeveelheid geld van de gemeente.

statements.title: For every tree that is chopped down in the municipality, two new ones should be planted.
statements.moreInfo: Op grond van de gemeente Tilburg staan ongeveer 105.000 bomen, de bossen niet meegerekend. Soms moet een boom weg, bijvoorbeeld als een boom ziek is. Op dit moment plant de gemeente daar 1 nieuwe boom voor terug, op dezelfde plaats of in de buurt.

statements.title: More large residential complexes for migrant workers should be built in industrial estates near the edge of town.
The municipality should cease maintenance on the Draaiend Huis (the Spinning House), an artwork on the Hasselt roundabout.
statements.moreInfo: Het kunstwerk Draaiend Huis is gemaakt door John Körmeling. Het staat op de Hasseltrotonde. Het kunstwerk is een vrijstaand rijtjeshuis dat meedraait over de rotonde, met de richting van het verkeer mee. Sinds eind 2024 staat het kunstwerk stil, omdat de technische installatie van het kunstwerk moet worden vervangen.

statements.title: The municipality should not allow any new cannabis cafés (coffeeshops).
Tilburg should abolish the citizens’ assembly, which advises the municipal council. Citizens’ assembly members are chosen at random.
No businesses or other buildings should be built in Wijkevoort between Tilburg and Gilze.
People should perform community service in return for their benefit payments.
The municipality leases land to farmers. When these contracts expire, the municipality should no longer lease the land to livestock farmers.
There should be more security cameras in Tilburg nightlife areas.
Every square in the town centre should have a free public toilet.
Refugees with residency permits should be prioritised for rented public housing.
Tilburg should introduce parking permits in more neighbourhoods.
Tilburg should abolish the low-emission zone that stops high-polluting cars from entering the city centre.
The municipality should prohibit dogs from roaming free in nature reserves and parks.
At least 40% of new builds in Tilburg should be social rental housing. In Tilburg, around 31% of homes are currently social rental housing.
Tilburg should have a Muslim cemetery.
The municipality should support local public broadcaster Omroep Tilburg.
The municipality should prohibit solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should only allow housing construction within existing residential zones.
The municipality should fund new residences where seniors can live together and receive care.
The municipality should pay vet bills for people on low incomes.
The municipality should always allow farmers to run a business alongside their farm, such as a shop or camping ground.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.
statements.moreInfo: Jongerenwerk is hulp en begeleiding voor jongeren, op straat en in een aantal jongerencentra in wijken. In Tilburg wordt dit gedaan door welzijnsorganisaties.

statements.title: The municipality should continue to ban smoking at bus stops.
The municipality should fund a night bus route between the city centre and the villages and towns.
